summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Travel Baggage Planner app. All core functionality working correctly with minor DOM interaction issue during rapid automated testing.

minor_issues.issue: DOM detachment during rapid automated clicking - elements become unattached when state updates occur quickly
minor_issues.impact: Only affects automated testing, not normal user interaction
minor_issues.workaround: Add longer delays between DOM updates or implement more robust element re-selection

passed_tests: Page loads correctly with all 10 airline chips visible (AA, DL, UA, FR, U2, LH, AF, EK, SQ, NH)
Airline selection creates flight cards with tier dropdown and bag counters
Airline deselection via chip click removes flight card
Airline removal via X button removes flight card and deselects chip
Tier dropdown changes affect included bags status and costs correctly
Bag counter increment/decrement works correctly (min 0, max 5)
Total cost calculation accurate across multiple airlines
Minimum dimensions table shows correct L/W/H and weight across selected airlines
Metric/Imperial toggle converts all dimensions and weights correctly
Dark/Light theme toggle works and persists visually
Per-airline cost breakdown shows correct individual costs with tier names
Route-dependent pricing note displays when airline has null pricing
Airlines with included bags (Emirates checked) show $0 cost when within included count
United Basic Economy correctly charges $45 for carry-on bags
Tier changes correctly update pricing (Basic Economy to Economy removes carry-on fees)
Maximum bag limit (5) enforced with disabled increment button
Minimum bag limit (0) enforced with disabled decrement button
Multiple airlines can be selected and managed simultaneously
Cost breakdown section updates correctly for each airline
